title: Merck — Overview
html:
Module 1: Merck Account Overview

Governing Thought

Merck & Co. is ZoomRx's fourth-largest account — a $1.8M base spanning 5 therapeutic areas, 10+ brands, and 10+ active project types across a partnership dating to 2015. The account's complexity stems not from depth within any single TA (unlike J&J's multi-layered oncology engagement) but from breadth across fundamentally different franchises: Keytruda's $31.7B oncology IO empire, HIV's IDVYNSO launch play, Enflonsia's competitive infant RSV battle, Prevymis's transplant expansion, and Gardasil's structural decline. Overlaying everything is a corporate transformation: Merck restructured into two business units (Merck Oncology and Merck Specialty, Pharma & Infectious Diseases) under a $3B cost-cutting mandate with 6,000 job reductions. This restructuring creates both fragmented buying centers and fresh budget allocations around new launches. Mastering the franchise portfolio, competitive dynamics, and the LOE-driven strategic urgency is table stakes for managing this relationship.

Source: Dave Johnson FY26 AO Plan (Apr 2026 update), TA knowledge docs, cross-study syntheses AO: Dave Johnson (Principal) AD: Melissa Weinstein FY25 Base: $1.8M FY26 Target: $2.1M (20% growth) FY26 YTD (3/31/26): $666K (32% of target) Gap to target: $766K | Weighted pipeline: $671K Vinoth's cluster: East 7 (Chanakya, Vidhya, Madhur)

The Merck Landscape: What You Need to Know

Merck at a Glance

Merck & Co. (known as MSD outside the US) is a global pharmaceutical company with 2025 revenue of ~$64B, of which Keytruda alone contributes ~$31.7B (~50%). The company is in the middle of a generational transition: the Keytruda LOE cliff (2028-2029) is forcing simultaneous franchise defense (QLEX product hop, perioperative indication expansion) and portfolio diversification (IDVYNSO in HIV, EyeBio in ophthalmology, PCSK-9 in cardiology, V940 cancer vaccine with Moderna).

Dimension Detail
Therapeutic Areas Oncology (Keytruda pan-tumor), HIV, Vaccines (Gardasil, Enflonsia, VAXELIS), Transplant (Prevymis), Pipeline (EyeBio, PCSK-9, Winrevair)
Brands we support 10+ (Keytruda, Keytruda QLEX, Pifeltro, IDVYNSO, Gardasil, Enflonsia, VAXELIS, Prevymis, Lynparza, and others)
Partnership tenure Since ~2015 (11 years)
Active project types PET, ATU, HCP-Patient Conversations, Message Testing, Digital Tracking, Qualitative TDIs, Listening Sessions, Global RT/Retainer, Sampling Studies, SFE
Corporate context Restructured into 2 BUs (Oncology + Specialty); $3B cost-cutting; 6,000 job reductions

Why it matters: Merck is simultaneously defending its largest franchise (Keytruda) and investing in 3+ new launches (IDVYNSO, KN-689, Enflonsia). Every MR dollar is scrutinized. ZoomRx must demonstrate ROI on every engagement while positioning for the launch-driven growth wave.

Account Strategy

Core approach: Focus on new launches (EyeBio, PCSK-9) and introducing capabilities/AI across all teams. Assure renewals/expansions across wide array of offerings; expand Keytruda indications.

1H26 Reality:

  • Merck announced restructuring into two BUs
  • $3B cost-cutting exercise negatively impacting MR spending
  • 1H26 bookings driven by oncology, vaccines (RSV), and HIV
  • Improvement starting late March — global RT win for HIV ($125K, first global win at Merck); Gardasil revisiting PET; Keytruda message testing proposals

Forecast: Growth expected in 2H26 but likely not enough to close gap. Expect stabilization for 2H26 and FY27.

Oncology Portfolio (Primary Revenue Driver)

Oncology generates the largest share of Merck project volume, dominated by Keytruda across multiple tumor types.

KEYTRUDA (pembrolizumab) — Pan-Tumor IO

  • What it is: Anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody; the world's top-selling oncology drug with ~$31.7B in 2025 global sales and 40+ approved indications across 20+ tumor types
  • Key indications: NSCLC (1L metastatic, perioperative KN-671), HNSCC (recurrent/metastatic KN-048, perioperative KN-689), melanoma (adjuvant/metastatic), urothelial (with Padcev), RCC, endometrial (with Lenvima), cervical, ovarian (new Feb 2026), MSI-H/dMMR tumors, and more
  • Revenue trajectory: $25.0B (FY23) -> $29.5B (FY24) -> $31.7B (FY25) -> decelerating growth; Q4 2025 at $8.4B
  • LOE timeline: Composition-of-matter patent expires 2028; IV biosimilar entry expected 2028-2029
  • Key competitors: Opdivo (BMS, ~$9.5B), Imfinzi (AstraZeneca, ~$4.5B), Tecentriq (Roche, ~$3.5B), Libtayo (Regeneron/Sanofi, ~$1B), Bavencio (EMD Serono/Pfizer, ~$0.7B)
  • ZoomRx work: NSCLC PET, HNSCC HCP-Pt + Qual + ATU + Listening Sessions, SubQ IO HCP-Pt, Message Testing, Lynparza mCRPC PET

Principal note: Keytruda is the franchise that underpins the entire account. The LOE cliff (2028) creates strategic urgency — every ZoomRx study should connect to Merck's LOE defense narrative (QLEX adoption, perioperative expansion, competitive defense).

KEYTRUDA QLEX (SubQ Formulation) — LOE Defense

  • What it is: First subcutaneous immune checkpoint inhibitor (pembrolizumab + berahyaluronidase alfa); administration in ~1 minute vs 30-minute IV infusion
  • Approved: September 2025; commercial launch late September 2025
  • Revenue: Q4 2025 sales $35M ($40M cumulative 2025) — <0.5% penetration after 3 months
  • J-code: Permanent HCPCS code J9277 effective April 1, 2026 — expected to significantly accelerate adoption
  • Adoption target: Merck expects 30-40% patient transition by 2028
  • Strategic importance: QLEX carries its own patent estate, potentially shielding the franchise from IV biosimilar competition beyond 2028
  • ZoomRx work: SubQ IO HCP-Patient Conversations (7 conversations collected as of Nov 2025)

Principal note: QLEX adoption tracking is potentially the most valuable research Merck's commercial team can get. If ZoomRx can quantify the adoption inflection — especially community vs. academic split and J-code impact — that positions ZoomRx as the LOE defense research partner.

KEYNOTE-689 (HNSCC Perioperative) — New Launch

  • What it is: Keytruda as neoadjuvant treatment + adjuvant treatment in resectable locally advanced HNSCC (Stage III-IVA); first perioperative IO approval in HNSCC
  • Approved: June 12, 2025 — first HNSCC approval in 6 years
  • Pivotal data: KN-689: EFS 51.8 vs 30.4 months (HR 0.70); pCR ~5-10%, mPR ~10-20%
  • Biomarker requirement: PD-L1 CPS >=1 via IHC
  • Competitive landscape: No other approved perioperative IO in LA HNSCC; Opdivo (NICO-RT trial results expected 2026-2027)
  • ZoomRx findings: Surgeon referral chain is the universal adoption bottleneck — not oncologist awareness. PD-L1 testing infrastructure immature: only 4/13 practices reflexive. 85% of surgeons present to MDT vs 65% perceived by oncologists. Community ONCs prescribe more (32% vs 15% academic — counterintuitive). ~50% of HCPs recall no new HNSCC treatments.
  • ZoomRx work: HCP-Pt Conversations + Qual, Biomarker Testing Qual, ATU W1, Listening Sessions (ongoing)

LYNPARZA (olaparib) — mCRPC

  • What it is: PARP inhibitor co-commercialized with AstraZeneca for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer
  • ZoomRx work: mCRPC PET (Project #40) — single project, limited growth potential

HIV Portfolio (Fastest-Growing Franchise)

IDVYNSO (doravirine/islatravir) — Upcoming Launch

  • What it is: First non-INSTI, tenofovir-free, once-daily complete 2-drug regimen with head-to-head Phase 3 data vs Biktarvy; first-in-class NRTTI (islatravir) + NNRTI (doravirine) combination
  • FDA approval: April 21, 2026; available in pharmacies from May 11, 2026
  • Initial indication: Switch-only (virologically suppressed adults); treatment-naive NDA expected based on CROI 2026 data
  • Key differentiators: Non-INSTI anchor (no weight gain class effect), tenofovir-free (no renal/bone concerns), 2-drug (fewer toxicities), novel MOA (high resistance barrier), favorable weight/lipid profile
  • Key risk: Islatravir's earlier development setback (CD4 decline at higher PrEP doses) creates perception headwind among ID specialists, even though the approved 0.25mg dose showed no clinically meaningful effect
  • Competitive landscape: Biktarvy (Gilead, ~$13B+, 45-50% share, 42% SOV) dominant; Dovato (ViiV/GSK) direct 2-drug competitor; Cabenuva (ViiV) injectable disruptor; Lenacapavir (Gilead) twice-yearly future threat
  • Revenue projection: ~$1.8B by 2033 (analyst consensus)
  • ZoomRx work: Global RT ($125K — first global Merck win), SFE tracking planned 2H 2026 (2 waves, N=90/wave)

Principal note: IDVYNSO is the central question for Merck's HIV franchise: can their #3 sales force (28% SOV) compete with Gilead's dominant presence (42% SOV)? The product data is strong — the execution infrastructure is the constraint. Every ZoomRx HIV study should measure whether the field force can pivot from Pifeltro's niche (lipids/renal/comorbidities) to IDVYNSO's platform play (head-to-head Biktarvy efficacy).

PIFELTRO (doravirine) & DELSTRIGO (doravirine/lamivudine/TDF) — Legacy

  • What they are: Pifeltro is a standalone NNRTI used in combination; Delstrigo is a 3-drug STR. Both being superseded by IDVYNSO.
  • Market position: Merck is #3 in HIV behind Gilead and ViiV; combined sub-$500M revenue
  • ZoomRx SFE data (Aug-Dec 2025): SOV 28%, reach ~65% monthly, rep closing 52% (on par with Dovato), visual aid usage 86%, avg interaction 16.3 min (longest in market)
  • Transition: Merck reps will promote ONLY DOR/ISL (IDVYNSO) from May 2026 — no more Pifeltro promotions

Vaccines Portfolio

GARDASIL 9 (HPV Vaccine)

  • What it is: 9-valent HPV vaccine; gold standard for cervical/genital cancer prevention
  • Revenue: $8.5B (FY24 peak) -> $5.2B (FY25, -39%) — driven by China collapse ($3.5B -> $193M)
  • Key dynamics: China shipments paused Feb 2025; domestic competitor launched May 2025; 2026 assumes $0 China revenue. CDC single-dose recommendation shifting (2-3 doses to 1, reducing per-patient revenue 50-67%). US franchise (~$2-3B annually) is stable.
  • ZoomRx work: PET (revisiting — historical VAXELIS PET ran 2021-2024)

Enflonsia (clesrovimab) — Infant RSV Prevention

  • What it is: Long-acting monoclonal antibody for prevention of RSV lower respiratory tract disease in infants; single 105mg IM dose regardless of weight
  • Approved: June 9, 2025; commercial launch July 2025
  • Key competitor: Beyfortus (Sanofi/AZ) — first-mover with 74.9% efficacy vs Enflonsia's 60.5%, 6M+ infants immunized, 40+ RWE studies
  • Enflonsia's edge: Dosing simplicity (weight-independent), storage superiority (30-month shelf life, 48hr room temp), supply reliability (no shortage history vs Beyfortus 2023 debacle)
  • Revenue: Launch year — both priced at $556/dose
  • ZoomRx work: PET (Q4'25, Q1'26), Digital Tracking (Q3'25, Q1'26)
  • Key finding: Enflonsia dosing message (ENF2: same dose regardless of weight) is 79% ME, 84% recall — the strongest message. But Beyfortus reach is 80% PP vs 50% Enflonsia. Only ~33% of HCPs report high likelihood to stock Enflonsia.

VAXELIS (Hexavalent Pediatric Vaccine) — Merck/Sanofi JV

  • What it is: 6-in-1 pediatric combination vaccine (DTaP-IPV-Hep B-Hib); reduces injection burden
  • ZoomRx work: Historical PET (4 waves, 2021-2024)

V940 (mRNA Cancer Vaccine) — Merck/Moderna

  • What it is: Individualized neoantigen therapy using patient-specific mRNA; administered with Keytruda
  • Data: KEYNOTE-942 5-year data: 49% reduction in recurrence/death in resected Stage III/IV melanoma
  • Trials: INTerpath-001 (Phase 3, melanoma, fully enrolled), INTerpath-002 (Phase 3, NSCLC), INTerpath-009 (Phase 3, NSCLC)
  • Revenue potential: $5-10B+ peak if approved across multiple solid tumors (highly speculative)
  • ZoomRx work: None yet — future opportunity for HCP-Pt, ATU, or launch tracking

Transplant

PREVYMIS (letermovir) — CMV Prophylaxis

  • What it is: First-in-class CMV DNA terminase complex inhibitor; approved for prophylaxis in allogeneic HSCT (2017) and high-risk D+/R- kidney transplant (2023); pediatric expansion 2024; extended 200-day dosing in high-risk HSCT
  • Revenue: $978M (FY25, +25%) — approaching $1B; KT expansion is the growth driver
  • Key differentiator vs generic valganciclovir: No myelosuppression (26% vs 64% leukopenia, P<0.001), lower discontinuation (4.1% vs 13.5%), zero resistance (0% vs 12.1%). But 15-30x cost premium (~$225/day vs ~$8-15/day generic).
  • Competitive landscape: Livtencity (maribavir, Takeda) — different niche (refractory CMV treatment, not prophylaxis)
  • ZoomRx work: KT ATU (Wave 2, 2024), KT PET/Message Recall (Wave 1, May-Aug 2025), KT Patient Qual
  • Key finding: KT7 ("No CMV cases with Prevymis vs. 5 cases with valganciclovir through Week 28") is the most effective message but least recalled — the biggest messaging opportunity. Prevymis leads market SOV.

Pipeline to Watch

These assets will generate new project requests as they advance. Flag them in account planning conversations.

Asset Mechanism Stage Why It Matters
EyeBio Ophthalmology (pipeline — details TBD) Pipeline New division, new contacts needed. Launch services (PET + ATU + Launch KPI) opportunity. Requires Rhonda Harp introduction.
PCSK-9 Cardiology (LDL-C lowering) Pipeline New therapeutic area for Merck. Launch timing uncertain.
Winrevair (sotatercept) Activin signaling inhibitor for PAH Launched 2024 Pricing workbook exists in BD folder. Growing franchise — tracking opportunity.
V940 Individualized mRNA cancer vaccine Phase 3 (3 trials) First-in-class; readout ~2027. Potential $5-10B+ franchise. ZoomRx should prepare ATU/launch tracking pitch.
IDVYNSO treatment-naive DOR/ISL for new HIV diagnoses NDA expected Label expansion from switch-only; broadens addressable market significantly.
Enflonsia second-season Clesrovimab for children <2yr at high risk Filing planned SMART trial positive; would match Beyfortus second-season indication.
Islatravir + Lenacapavir Oral once-weekly HIV treatment Phase 3 (ISLEND) Merck/Gilead collaboration; if successful, IDVYNSO becomes bridge product to weekly oral.

ZoomRx's Service Footprint

Project Type Keytruda (pan-tumor) HIV (DOR/ISL) Enflonsia (RSV) Prevymis (Transplant) Gardasil (HPV) Lynparza (mCRPC)
PET X (NSCLC) — X X Revisiting X
ATU X (HNSCC W1) — — X (Kidney) — —
HCP-Patient Conversations X (KN-689, SubQ) — — — — —
Message Testing X (Global, V2 pipeline) — — — — —
SFE Tracking — X (planned 2H26) — — — —
Digital Tracking — — X — — —
Global RT/Retainer — X ($125K) — — — —
Qualitative TDIs X (Biomarker, KN-689) — — X (KT Patient) — —
Listening Sessions X (KN-689 ongoing) — — — — —
Sampling Study — X (Jul 2025) — — — —
MSL Tracking Opportunity — — — — —
Social Listening Opportunity — — — — —
Launch KPI N/A — — — — —

Key takeaway: Keytruda runs the widest project portfolio (7 project types). HIV is the fastest-growing franchise on the account with launch-driven work ahead. Enflonsia and Prevymis are steady but narrow (2-3 types each). The service footprint is significantly narrower per brand than J&J — expansion within existing brands (adding MSL tracking, social listening, launch KPI) is the most efficient growth path.

Competitive Landscape Summary

Brand Primary Competitor Competitive Dynamic Implication for ZoomRx Work
Keytruda (metastatic) Opdivo (BMS), Imfinzi (AZ), Tecentriq (Roche) Keytruda dominates with broadest label (40+ indications) but faces deceleration and LOE in 2028 PET tracks competitive SOV; message testing measures differentiation against IO competitors
Keytruda QLEX IV biosimilars (2028-2029 entry) Product-hop LOE defense; <0.5% penetration after 3 months; J-code April 2026 may accelerate SubQ IO study directly measures adoption dynamics — high strategic value
KN-689 (HNSCC periop) Opdivo NICO-RT (results 2026-2027) First-mover advantage; no other approved perioperative IO in LA HNSCC ATU and HCP-Pt track launch curve; competitive monitoring needed if Opdivo data read out positively
IDVYNSO Biktarvy (Gilead, 42% SOV), Dovato (ViiV) First non-INSTI 2-drug STR vs Biktarvy; structural SOV disadvantage (28% vs 42%) SFE tracking (2H26) measures whether Merck's field force can compete with Gilead's dominant presence
Enflonsia Beyfortus (Sanofi/AZ) Beyfortus has efficacy edge (74.9% vs 60.5%) + first-mover + 6M infants; Enflonsia has dosing/storage edge PET tracks competitive reach, SOV, stocking likelihood; digital tracks online presence gap
Prevymis Generic valganciclovir 15-30x cost premium but dramatically better tolerability (26% vs 64% leukopenia); cost barrier is real but manageable Message recall study tracks whether compelling efficacy messages (KT7) are being delivered
Gardasil Chinese domestic 9-valent; single-dose CDC guidance China revenue collapsed; US stable but volume pressured by dose reduction If PET reactivates, tracks HCP behavior change around single-dose guidance

Org Structure

  • Restructured into Merck Oncology and Merck Specialty, Pharma & Infectious Diseases
  • Rhonda Harp runs all of Merck Insights — the single most important relationship on the account
  • Follows closely with MSA and preferred vendor lists — ZoomRx well positioned
  • Silos exist within the company — ongoing challenge exacerbated by BU split
  • $3B cost-cutting creating budget pressure on MR across all franchises

AI Status

  • At MR levels, not aware of broader Merck AI initiatives
  • Pitched AI across teams using Dynamic Dialogues — interest shown but not adopted more deeply
  • On Keytruda, pitched Sagan follow-up quals but need AI addendum in MSA (working with procurement)
  • AI addendum is the #1 blocker for ZoomRx's differentiated capabilities at Merck
  • Until resolved, ZoomRx cannot sell Dynamic Dialogues, Sagan, AI-powered moderation, or any AI-differentiated work
  • Competitors without MSA restrictions could establish AI positioning first

Key Relationships

  • Relationships at Director level throughout Merck, plus AD and manager levels
  • Rhonda Harp — runs all of Merck Insights. Introductory meeting held, working to expand for capabilities.
  • Day-to-day contacts across oncology (Evan, Ted, Allison, Rachel, Adit Desai, Anil Melathe), vaccines, HIV, transplant teams
  • ZoomRx team: Dave Johnson (AO), Melissa Weinstein (AD), East 7 cluster (Chanakya, Vidhya, Madhur), plus Jane, Stuti, Kopal, Revanth, David, Abhishek, Alex Colborn

The Bottom Line

Merck is an account at an inflection point. The $1.8M base is solid but the 20% growth target is ambitious in a cost-cutting environment. Three factors will determine FY26 outcome: (1) whether renewals hold across all franchises (the floor), (2) whether IDVYNSO and KN-689 launch tracking converts to sustained revenue (the growth vector), and (3) whether the AI MSA addendum gets resolved, unlocking ZoomRx's most differentiated capabilities (the ceiling). Vinoth's contribution: flawless East 7 execution, strategic support on expansion pitches, and capacity management as new wins arrive.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home

Governing Thought

The account's complexity stems not from depth within any single TA (unlike J&J's multi-layered oncology engagement) but from breadth across fundamentally different franchises: Keytruda's $31.7B oncology IO empire, HIV's IDVYNSO launch play, Enflonsia's competitive infant RSV battle, Prevymis's transplant expansion, and Gardasil's structural decline. Overlaying everything is a corporate transformation: Merck restructured into two business units (Merck Oncology and Merck Specialty, Pharma & Infectious Diseases) under a $3B cost-cutting mandate with 6,000 job reductions. Mastering the franchise portfolio, competitive dynamics, and the LOE-driven strategic urgency is table stakes for managing this relationship.

AD: Melissa Weinstein

FY26 Target: $2.1M (20% growth)
FY26 YTD (3/31/26): $666K (32% of target)

KEYNOTE-689 (HNSCC Perioperative) — New Launch

Approved: June 12, 2025 — first HNSCC approval in 6 years

Biomarker requirement: PD-L1 CPS >=1 via IHC

ZoomRx findings: Surgeon referral chain is the universal adoption bottleneck — not oncologist awareness. PD-L1 testing infrastructure immature: only 4/13 practices reflexive. 85% of surgeons present to MDT vs 65% perceived by oncologists. Community ONCs prescribe more (32% vs 15% academic — counterintuitive). ~50% of HCPs recall no new HNSCC treatments.
ZoomRx work: HCP-Pt Conversations + Qual, Biomarker Testing Qual, ATU W1, Listening Sessions (ongoing)

LYNPARZA (olaparib) — mCRPC

What it is: PARP inhibitor co-commercialized with AstraZeneca for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer
ZoomRx work: mCRPC PET (Project #40) — single project, limited growth potential


HIV Portfolio (Fastest-Growing Franchise)
IDVYNSO (doravirine/islatravir) — Upcoming Launch

What it is: First non-INSTI, tenofovir-free, once-daily complete 2-drug regimen with head-to-head Phase 3 data vs Biktarvy; first-in-class NRTTI (islatravir) + NNRTI (doravirine) combination
FDA approval: April 21, 2026; available in pharmacies from May 11, 2026
Initial indication: Switch-only (virologically suppressed adults); treatment-naive NDA expected based on CROI 2026 data
Key differentiators: Non-INSTI anchor (no weight gain class effect), tenofovir-free (no renal/bone concerns), 2-drug (fewer toxicities), novel MOA (high resistance barrier), favorable weight/lipid profile
Key risk: Islatravir's earlier development setback (CD4 decline at higher PrEP doses) creates perception headwind among ID specialists, even though the approved 0.25mg dose showed no clinically meaningful effect
Competitive landscape: Biktarvy (Gilead, ~$13B+, 45-50% share, 42% SOV) dominant; Dovato (ViiV/GSK) direct 2-drug competitor; Cabenuva (ViiV) injectable disruptor; Lenacapavir (Gilead) twice-yearly future threat
Revenue projection: ~$1.8B by 2033 (analyst consensus)
ZoomRx work: Global RT ($125K — first global Merck win), SFE tracking planned 2H 2026 (2 waves, N=90/wave)


Principal note: IDVYNSO is the central question for Merck's HIV franchise: can their #3 sales force (28% SOV) compete with Gilead's dominant presence (42% SOV)? The product data is strong — the execution infrastructure is the constraint. Every ZoomRx HIV study should measure whether the field force can pivot from Pifeltro's niche (lipids/renal/comorbidities) to IDVYNSO's platform play (head-to-head Biktarvy efficacy).

PIFELTRO (doravirine) & DELSTRIGO (doravirine/lamivudine/TDF) — Legacy

What they are: Pifeltro is a standalone NNRTI used in combination; Delstrigo is a 3-drug STR. Both being superseded by IDVYNSO.
Market position: Merck is #3 in HIV behind Gilead and ViiV; combined sub-$500M revenue
ZoomRx SFE data (Aug-Dec 2025): SOV 28%, reach ~65% monthly, rep closing 52% (on par with Dovato), visual aid usage 86%, avg interaction 16.3 min (longest in market)
Transition: Merck reps will promote ONLY DOR/ISL (IDVYNSO) from May 2026 — no more Pifeltro promotions


Vaccines Portfolio
GARDASIL 9 (HPV Vaccine)

What it is: 9-valent HPV vaccine; gold standard for cervical/genital cancer prevention
Revenue: $8.5B (FY24 peak) -> $5.2B (FY25, -39%) — driven by China collapse ($3.5B -> $193M)
Key dynamics: China shipments paused Feb 2025; domestic competitor launched May 2025; 2026 assumes $0 China revenue. CDC single-dose recommendation shifting (2-3 doses to 1, reducing per-patient revenue 50-67%). US franchise (~$2-3B annually) is stable.
ZoomRx work: PET (revisiting — historical VAXELIS PET ran 2021-2024)

Enflonsia (clesrovimab) — Infant RSV Prevention

What it is: Long-acting monoclonal antibody for prevention of RSV lower respiratory tract disease in infants; single 105mg IM dose regardless of weight
Approved: June 9, 2025; commercial launch July 2025
Key competitor: Beyfortus (Sanofi/AZ) — first-mover with 74.9% efficacy vs Enflonsia's 60.5%, 6M+ infants immunized, 40+ RWE studies
Enflonsia's edge: Dosing simplicity (weight-independent), storage superiority (30-month shelf life, 48hr room temp), supply reliability (no shortage history vs Beyfortus 2023 debacle)
Revenue: Launch year — both priced at $556/dose
ZoomRx work: PET (Q4'25, Q1'26), Digital Tracking (Q3'25, Q1'26)
Key finding: Enflonsia dosing message (ENF2: same dose regardless of weight) is 79% ME, 84% recall — the strongest message. But Beyfortus reach is 80% PP vs 50% Enflonsia. Only ~33% of HCPs report high likelihood to stock Enflonsia.

VAXELIS (Hexavalent Pediatric Vaccine) — Merck/Sanofi JV

What it is: 6-in-1 pediatric combination vaccine (DTaP-IPV-Hep B-Hib); reduces injection burden
ZoomRx work: Historical PET (4 waves, 2021-2024)

V940 (mRNA Cancer Vaccine) — Merck/Moderna

What it is: Individualized neoantigen therapy using patient-specific mRNA; administered with Keytruda
Data: KEYNOTE-942 5-year data: 49% reduction in recurrence/death in resected Stage III/IV melanoma
Trials: INTerpath-001 (Phase 3, melanoma, fully enrolled), INTerpath-002 (Phase 3, NSCLC), INTerpath-009 (Phase 3, NSCLC)
Revenue potential: $5-10B+ peak if approved across multiple solid tumors (highly speculative)
ZoomRx work: None yet — future opportunity for HCP-Pt, ATU, or launch tracking


Transplant
PREVYMIS (letermovir) — CMV Prophylaxis

What it is: First-in-class CMV DNA terminase complex inhibitor; approved for prophylaxis in allogeneic HSCT (2017) and high-risk D+/R- kidney transplant (2023); pediatric expansion 2024; extended 200-day dosing in high-risk HSCT
Revenue: $978M (FY25, +25%) — approaching $1B; KT expansion is the growth driver
Key differentiator vs generic valganciclovir: No myelosuppression (26% vs 64% leukopenia, P<0.001), lower discontinuation (4.1% vs 13.5%), zero resistance (0% vs 12.1%). But 15-30x cost premium (~$225/day vs ~$8-15/day generic).
Competitive landscape: Livtencity (maribavir, Takeda) — different niche (refractory CMV treatment, not prophylaxis)
ZoomRx work: KT ATU (Wave 2, 2024), KT PET/Message Recall (Wave 1, May-Aug 2025), KT Patient Qual
Key finding: KT7 ("No CMV cases with Prevymis vs. 5 cases with valganciclovir through Week 28") is the most effective message but least recalled — the biggest messaging opportunity. Prevymis leads market SOV.

Key takeaway: Keytruda runs the widest project portfolio (7 project types). HIV is the fastest-growing franchise on the account with launch-driven work ahead. Enflonsia and Prevymis are steady but narrow (2-3 types each). The service footprint is significantly narrower per brand than J&J — expansion within existing brands (adding MSL tracking, social listening, launch KPI) is the most efficient growth path.

Competitive Landscape Summary



Brand
Primary Competitor
Competitive Dynamic
Implication for ZoomRx Work




Keytruda (metastatic)
Opdivo (BMS), Imfinzi (AZ), Tecentriq (Roche)
Keytruda dominates with broadest label (40+ indications) but faces deceleration and LOE in 2028
PET tracks competitive SOV; message testing measures differentiation against IO competitors


Keytruda QLEX
IV biosimilars (2028-2029 entry)
Product-hop LOE defense; <0.5% penetration after 3 months; J-code April 2026 may accelerate
SubQ IO study directly measures adoption dynamics — high strategic value


KN-689 (HNSCC periop)
Opdivo NICO-RT (results 2026-2027)
First-mover advantage; no other approved perioperative IO in LA HNSCC
ATU and HCP-Pt track launch curve; competitive monitoring needed if Opdivo data read out positively


IDVYNSO
Biktarvy (Gilead, 42% SOV), Dovato (ViiV)
First non-INSTI 2-drug STR vs Biktarvy; structural SOV disadvantage (28% vs 42%)
SFE tracking (2H26) measures whether Merck's field force can compete with Gilead's dominant presence


Enflonsia
Beyfortus (Sanofi/AZ)
Beyfortus has efficacy edge (74.9% vs 60.5%) + first-mover + 6M infants; Enflonsia has dosing/storage edge
PET tracks competitive reach, SOV, stocking likelihood; digital tracks online presence gap


Prevymis
Generic valganciclovir
15-30x cost premium but dramatically better tolerability (26% vs 64% leukopenia); cost barrier is real but manageable
Message recall study tracks whether compelling efficacy messages (KT7) are being delivered


Gardasil
Chinese domestic 9-valent; single-dose CDC guidance
China revenue collapsed; US stable but volume pressured by dose reduction
If PET reactivates, tracks HCP behavior change around single-dose guidance




Org Structure

Restructured into Merck Oncology and Merck Specialty, Pharma & Infectious Diseases
Rhonda Harp runs all of Merck Insights — the single most important relationship on the account
Follows closely with MSA and preferred vendor lists — ZoomRx well positioned
Silos exist within the company — ongoing challenge exacerbated by BU split
$3B cost-cutting creating budget pressure on MR across all franchises


AI Status

At MR levels, not aware of broader Merck AI initiatives
Pitched AI across teams using Dynamic Dialogues — interest shown but not adopted more deeply
On Keytruda, pitched Sagan follow-up quals but need AI addendum in MSA (working with procurement)
AI addendum is the #1 blocker for ZoomRx's differentiated capabilities at Merck
Until resolved, ZoomRx cannot sell Dynamic Dialogues, Sagan, AI-powered moderation, or any AI-differentiated work
Competitors without MSA restrictions could establish AI positioning first
